content.full_text:
Re
Fern PolrrlcAt- PRACTTcES CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
November 5,2003
Robert W. Bell
Bob Bell Real Estate
728 East Katella
Orange, C492867
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-03-251
Dear Mr. Bell:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act").1 In a telephone conversation
with Commission staff on October 29,2003, you confirmed that your question was
whether you made, participated in making, or attempted to influence a governmental
decision that took place on July 22,2003. Because your question concerns past conduct,
we are unable to provide written advice. (See Govt. Code g 8311a@); regulation
18329(bX8)(A) & (C), copy enclosed.)
I enclosed a copy of the pamphlet entitled Can I Vote: Overview of the Conflicts
Laws, which may assist you with your question.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
HBAjg

I Government Code sections 81000 - g7}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question: Whether you made, participated in making, or attempted to influence a governmental decision that took place on July 22, 2003.
sections.conclusion: Because your question concerns past conduct, we are unable to provide written advice. (See Govt. Code § 8311a(b); regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) & (C), copy enclosed.)
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether he made, participated in making, or attempted to influence a governmental decision on July 22, 2003, under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the question concerns past conduct, which falls outside the scope of the Commission's advisory authority under Government Code § 8311a(b) and regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) & (C).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter where FPPC declines to provide advice on past conduct. The question and conclusion are clearly stated in the body text rather than in formal sections. Document references Government Code § 8311a(b) as the statutory basis for the declination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether you made, participated in making, or attempted to influence a governmental decision that took place on July 22, 2003.

CONCLUSION: Because your question concerns past conduct, we are unable to provide written advice. (See Govt. Code § 8311a(b); regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) & (C), copy enclosed.)

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Robert W. Bell regarding whether he influenced a governmental decision on July 22, 2003, because the question concerns past conduct which the Commission cannot advise upon in writing.
